title: Vheh'yaim
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1|notoc=1}}
[[File:Vhehyaim-Insider86.jpg|thumb|right|320px|A traditional Mandalorian ''vheh'yaim'']]
A '''''vheh'yaim''''' was a traditional [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] dwelling, designed to be easily built for temporary living quarters, whether "temporary" amounted to a single night or several [[Standard year/Legends|years]].<ref name="TMPaC">{{InsiderCite|86|The Mandalorians: People and Culture}}</ref> ''Vheh'yaime'' varied in size; a small ''vheh'yaim'', measuring anywhere from three to fifteen [[Meter/Legends|meters]] across, was known as an '''''akaata'yaim'''''.<ref name="FLT">''[[Friends Like These]]''</ref> Circular and dome-like in shape, ''vheh'yaime'' were often constructed out of [[Color/Legends|green]] wood, woven with [[Vine/Legends|vines]] and matted with mud,<ref name="TMPaC" /> [[grass]], and [[Tree/Legends|tree]] bark, providing natural camouflage.<ref name="FLT" /> A typical ''vheh'yaim'' was partially submerged in the ground,<ref name="TC">''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]''</ref> with a sunken entrance and a cut-in stairwell accessway.<ref name="FLT" /> ''Vheh'yaime'' frequently included an [[Armory/Legends|armory]], kitchen, and [[Medical bay/Legends|infirmary]], in addition to living quarters.<ref name="FLT" /><ref name="TC" />

The Mandalorians of [[Clan Awaud/Legends|Clan Awaud]] constructed a large ''vheh'yaim'', fifty meters wide, for their clan's senior members and [[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] to reside in. Deemed "[[Clan Home]]," it was joined by numerous smaller ''akaata'yaim'' in the settlement of [[Arumorut/Legends|Arumorut]] on [[Vlemoth Port/Legends|Vlemoth Port]].<ref name="FLT" /> [[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]], the home of [[Clan Skirata]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] in the years following the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], was another example of a large ''vheh'yaim''.<ref name="TC" /> [[Standard decade/Legends|Decades]] later, during the time of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], several ''vheh'yaime'' were also set up across [[Beviin-Vasur farm|the farm]] of Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] [[Goran Beviin]] and [[Medrit Vasur]], near the Mandalorian [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], in addition to the main [[Stone/Legends|stone]] [[Farm/Legends|farmhouse]]. These outbuildings were interconnected by underground tunnels.<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The ''vheh'yaim'' was first introduced to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe in [[Karen Traviss]]' ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article ''[[The Mandalorians: People and Culture]]'', published in [[February]] of [[2006]] as part of the magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 86|eight-sixth issue]].<ref name="TMPaC" /> The following year, Traviss included a large ''vheh'yaim'' in the form of Kyrimorut in the third entry in her ''[[Republic Commando]]'' novel series, [[Republic Commando: True Colors|''True Colors'']].<ref name="TC" /> [[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel|''Order 66'']] and ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'', the series' sequels to ''True Colors'', both featured further appearances of the Kyrimorut ''vheh'yaim''.<ref name="O66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref><ref name="ImpCom501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref> Traviss later included several ''vheh'yaime'' in two of her contributions to the multi-[[Author/Legends|author]] ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' series, [[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice|''Sacrifice'']]<ref name="Sacrifice" /> and [[Legacy of the Force: Revelation|''Revelation'']].<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref> The [[roleplaying game]] adventure book ''[[Friends Like These]]'', published on [[December 8]], [[2016]] by [[Fantasy Flight Games]], also featured ''vheh'yaime''. In addition, ''Friends Like These'' introduced the term "''akaata'yaim''," a designation for smaller ''vheh'yaime''.<ref name="FLT" />
